company.name: LangChain
company.notes: Agent orchestration framework (you're learning their tools). Brand-strong.

research.status_summary: Actively hiring and in strong growth mode — no layoffs, no acquisition; raised $125M Series B (Oct 2025) at $1.25B valuation with ~304 employees as of April 2026.
research.hiring_policy: LangChain self-describes as a small, remote-friendly team with roles across SF, Austin, Charlotte, NYC, and London; visa sponsorship is rare in the broader LangChain ecosystem (tracked at ~3.3% of agentic-AI listings industry-wide) and no explicit EOR policy is advertised, making direct sponsorship a confirm-first item.
recent_news: March 2026: Comprehensive NVIDIA enterprise partnership announced — LangChain joined the Nemotron Coalition and combined LangSmith + Deep Agents + LangGraph with NVIDIA Agent Toolkit, Nemotron models, and NVIDIA Dynamo for a full-stack enterprise agent platform.

March 2026: LangSmith Fleet launch (rebrand of Agent Builder) added agent identity management, sandboxed execution environments, RBAC/ABAC controls, and 7,500+ Arcade.dev tools — targeting enterprise production deployments.

May 2026 (Interrupt conference): Shipped LangSmith Engine (autonomous trace monitoring + PR-generating fixes), Managed Deep Agents, SmithDB (92ms P50 trace loads, 15x perf gain), Context Hub, and LLM Gateway — all aimed at closing the prototype-to-production gap.
April 2026: State of Agent Engineering report (1,300+ respondents) found 57% of orgs now have agents in production (up from 51%), with quality (32%) and latency (20%) as top barriers.
DataCamp partnership (March 2026) to launch an AI Engineering learning track, broadening LangChain's developer mindshare.
research.growth_signal: Headcount ~304 (Apr 2026, Tracxn), $260M total funding, $1.25–1.5B valuation, 1B+ cumulative framework downloads, 100M monthly downloads, and 300+ enterprise LangSmith customers — all pointing to rapid upward trajectory.
research.fit_for_lior: LangChain is the dominant open-source agent orchestration layer (LangGraph, Deep Agents, LangSmith) that underpins much of the Creative-AI and vibe-coding toolchain Lior works in, making it a high-relevance target for a Product Analyst or Deployed/Solutions Engineer role bridging AI product intuition with hands-on agent-building.
